checks.a_analysis: The HTML document faithfully translates the visual organization of the source PDF into a responsive, single-column web layout. Sections, headings, and lists follow the same sequence and hierarchical structure established in the original document. The transition from a paginated format to a continuous scroll does not disrupt the intended flow of information. Key structural elements like the Executive Summary, the numbering of goals, and the appendices are all placed where a user would expect them based on the source.

The document contains numerous tables, including simple data lists (e.g., Table 1), complex financial spreadsheets (FY 2021-2022 and FY 2022-2023), and structural goal-setting tables (Goals 1-10). All tables observed in the HTML screenshot are correctly rendered as tabular structures. They maintain distinct headers, row/column alignment, and borders. Even the highly dense financial tables in the middle of the document and the extensive population data in Appendix B are clearly legible and functionally identical to the source presentation.

All meaningful visual assets from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. This includes the North Carolina flag photograph and the Ritter Strategic Services, LLC logo on the cover page, as well as the 'Figure 1 – Simplified NENA i3 NG911 Conceptual System Diagram' found in section 3.2. No broken image icons or missing meaningful graphics were detected.

A thorough visual scan of the HTML output confirms that no body content (paragraphs, tables, or diagrams) has been duplicated. While headings appear in both the Table of Contents and the body of the document, this is standard accessible design and does not constitute content duplication. The narrative text and data tables appear exactly once in their logical sequence.
